summary: SKGLSurfaceView freezes the Android app when returning from background because SKGLSurfaceViewRenderer.OnSurfaceCreated is empty and does not reset the stale GRContext after the EGL context is destroyed and recreated.

bugSignals.errorMessage: App freezes when returning from background with SKGLSurfaceView in a fragment

stepsToReproduce: Add SKGLSurfaceView inside a Fragment on Android
Send the app to the background
Return the app to the foreground
reproEvidence.environmentDetails: SkiaSharp 2.80.2, Android 11, Google Pixel 4a (5G), Visual Studio for Mac

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: The OnSurfaceCreated method in SKGLSurfaceViewRenderer is still empty in the current codebase. The bug has not been fixed.

analysis.summary: SKGLSurfaceViewRenderer.OnSurfaceCreated is empty and does not call FreeContext() to reset the GRContext when Android recreates the EGL context after the app resumes from background. The sibling class SKGLTextureViewRenderer correctly calls FreeContext() in its OnSurfaceCreated. The stale GRContext references a destroyed GL context, causing Skia GL operations to hang/freeze on the next OnDrawFrame.
analysis.rationale: Classified as type/bug because the app freezes during a normal Android lifecycle event (background/foreground). The root cause is a missing cleanup call in OnSurfaceCreated that the TextureView variant already handles correctly. The reporter's description is minimal but the code investigation clearly confirms the defect.

keySignals.text: Using a SKGLSurfaceView freezes the app when returning from the background
keySignals.interpretation: Android destroys the EGL context on background; freeze on resume confirms stale GL state.

keySignals.text: In my case, it is added inside a fragment
keySignals.interpretation: Fragment lifecycle may not automatically call GLSurfaceView.onPause/onResume, compounding the issue.
The code path has not changed since this version — the bug persists in the current codebase.

codeInvestigation.finding: OnSurfaceCreated is empty — does NOT call FreeContext() to reset the GRContext when the EGL context is recreated after resuming from background. This leaves a stale GRContext referencing a destroyed GL context.
OnSurfaceCreated calls FreeContext() to properly reset the GRContext when the EGL context is recreated. This is the correct behavior that SKGLSurfaceViewRenderer is missing.

OnDrawFrame only creates a new GRContext if context == null. Since OnSurfaceCreated doesn't null out the context, the stale GRContext is reused with the new (different) EGL context, causing GL operations to hang.

workarounds: Call SKGLSurfaceView.OnPause() in the Fragment/Activity onPause and SKGLSurfaceView.OnResume() in onResume to properly manage the GL lifecycle.
Switch to SKCanvasView (CPU-based rendering) if GPU acceleration is not critical.
nextQuestions: Does calling OnPause/OnResume from the fragment lifecycle fully resolve the freeze, or is the missing FreeContext the sole root cause?
Should SKGLSurfaceViewRenderer.OnSurfaceCreated call FreeContext() to match SKGLTextureViewRenderer?

resolution.hypothesis: When Android destroys and recreates the EGL context (background/foreground transition), SKGLSurfaceViewRenderer.OnSurfaceCreated does not reset the GRContext. The stale GRContext tries to use the destroyed GL context, causing Skia GL calls to hang.

proposals.title: Add FreeContext to OnSurfaceCreated
proposals.description: Add a FreeContext() call to SKGLSurfaceViewRenderer.OnSurfaceCreated, matching the pattern already used in SKGLTextureViewRenderer. This ensures the GRContext is reset when Android recreates the EGL context.

proposals.title: Call OnPause/OnResume from lifecycle
proposals.description: As a workaround, call SKGLSurfaceView.OnPause() from the hosting Fragment or Activity's onPause callback, and SKGLSurfaceView.OnResume() from onResume. This is standard Android GLSurfaceView lifecycle management.

proposals.title: Switch to SKCanvasView
proposals.description: If GPU acceleration is not critical, replace SKGLSurfaceView with SKCanvasView which uses CPU-based raster rendering and does not depend on the EGL context lifecycle.

resolution.recommendedReason: Trivial one-line fix that matches the existing correct pattern in SKGLTextureViewRenderer. Addresses the root cause rather than working around it.

actionability.reason: Code investigation reveals a clear defect (empty OnSurfaceCreated vs the correct implementation in TextureViewRenderer), but a proper fix needs testing on Android to confirm the hypothesis.

actions.description: Apply bug, views, and Android labels
Post analysis with workaround and root cause hypothesis
